sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a history of a knee injury sustained while skiing in Vermont last weekend.
She reports that her knee popped and has been painful ever since.
She was unable to bear weight and had to be taken down the mountain by the ski patrol.
The patient describes pain on the outside of her knee and a feeling that it won't hold her weight.
She is unable to bend her knee fully and experiences pain with straightening.
She denies numbness or tingling in her toes and is able to feel her toes.
She is generally healthy and denies any high blood pressure or diabetes.
The patient has twins and is a stay-at-home mom.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: I prescribed ibuprofen 600-800mg every 8 hours as needed for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The examination of the right knee reveals edema and an effusion.
There is pain to palpation of the right lateral knee, decreased flexion, and extension.
The Lachman sign is positive.
The dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses are palpable, and there is no leg edema in the ankle.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely has a ruptured anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) in her right knee, which is confirmed by the physical exam.
Problem list: ACL rupture; right knee pain; right knee edema; right knee effusion.
Plan:
I ordered a knee MRI to confirm the diagnosis.
I instructed the patient to continue taking ibuprofen 600-800mg every 8 hours as needed for pain.
I recommended a knee brace to provide additional support.
I scheduled a follow-up appointment to discuss the results of the MRI and determine the next steps in treatment.
Follow-up: The patient will return in a few days for a follow-up appointment to discuss the MRI results and determine the next steps in treatment.
